svg.text: Master Architecture · Azure-first .NET 10 BFF + Angular 21 SPA · Clean Architecture + CQRS · DB-first Persistence · Config-driven Multi-domain Layout

v2026.04.29 · branch: feature/db-first-pivot · scope: full-stack including Domain → Infrastructure → Application → API → BFF → Web (Angular) → Identity (Entra ID) → Data (Azure SQL) → Observability (App Insights / Serilog / OpenTelemetry)

g.text: 7 backend projects + 1 Angular ClientApp · Clean Architecture · CPM
├── src/Domain/ · entities, aggregates, value objects, events
├── src/Infrastructure/ · EF Core, repositories, external clients
└── src/Tools/DbMigrator/ · console app · "schema is source of truth"
src/app/core/ · auth, http, services, errors, telemetry plumbing
src/app/shared/ · DPH UI Kit (24 components) + layout chrome
src/app/features/ · vertical slices per aggregate (users, dashboard, …)
A.2 · REQUEST LIFECYCLE — browser → BFF → API → DB
Cookie session at the BFF · JWT only between BFF and API · single-resource OIDC
CorrelationId · Serilog structured logs · OpenTelemetry traces+metrics · Audit-writer · PII redaction · Polly retry/circuit-breaker (HTTP/gRPC) · Antiforgery @ BFF only
Every span/log/metric is tagged with the same correlation id end-to-end (browser → BFF → API → DB) so a single trace ID lets ops follow a request from click to row-update.
MapFallback uses "/{**catchAll}" not the single-arg overload — the default {*path:nonfile} constraint excludes /assets/<hash>.js (memory: feedback_mapfallback_nonfile_constraint).
⑤.a Application · MediatR pipeline behaviors (registration order)
Read queries skip UnitOfWork (no SaveChanges); they go through ReadDbContextAdapter which guarantees AsNoTracking — no accidental mutation drift.
All non-2xx return RFC 7807 ProblemDetails; the SPA's error interceptor maps them to NotificationService toasts.
WDAC note: Application Control on the dev box blocks dotnet run + WebApplicationFactory tests; build + unit tests still work (memory: feedback_wdac_blocks_runtime).
Single-registration · OIDC code+PKCE · BFF cookie · refresh_token-based Graph add-on
1. GET / → BFF redirects to Entra /authorize?client_id=…&scope=openid profile offline_access api://…/.default
2. User authenticates · MFA · consent (silent on subsequent visits)
3. Entra → BFF /signin-oidc with auth code → BFF exchanges for id_token + access_token + refresh_token
4. BFF issues encrypted cookie to browser; tokens NEVER leave the server (no localStorage / sessionStorage)
5. SPA calls /api/<endpoint> with cookie → BFF proxy attaches Bearer <access_token> → API verifies signature
Entra refuses to mint a token covering Graph scopes AND custom api://<app-id> scopes in the same request
(memory: feedback_entra_one_resource_per_token). Solution: acquire api://… at login; on first Graph call,
use the stored refresh_token to silently mint a Graph-scoped token via on-behalf-of flow. Cached for the cookie lifetime.
• Expose API · scope: api://<client-id>/access_as_user · authorized client = SAME app id
• MSAL-protected map keys must end in /* — regex-style match, not prefix (memory)
! NEVER ToDictionary raw Claims — Entra returns amr/roles/groups as MULTIPLE claims with the same Type. GroupBy(Type) first.
Web.UI Setup/ · Middleware/ · Endpoints/ · Services/ · Observability/
BFF folder structure (post-2026-04-23 refactor — dropped Bff* prefix)
└── appsettings.json · Auth · Proxy · SpaHosting · OTel · Serilog
Build is silent; failure surfaces only at request time (memory: feedback_addcontrollerswithviews_antiforgery).
• Optimistic concurrency on AuditableEntity (rowversion / xmin) → 409 Conflict on stale write
• Polly retry + circuit-breaker on every IHttpClientFactory + IGraphServiceClient call
• EF: .Include() or AsSplitQuery() required for >1 collection nav (N+1 guard)
• DbContextPool: scoped ambient services used in OnModelCreating / interceptors must be Singleton (memory)
• CA1716 — VB-interop reserved-keyword check (irrelevant; no VB callers)
• CA1852 escalated to error in Phase 2 (seal internal types by default)
• CA2007 escalated to error in Phase 2 (force ConfigureAwait on lib code)
Schema is source of truth · DbMigrator · scaffolded entities · DtoGen
Mapster fully removed in this pivot — DTOs are generated, not mapped at runtime. IUserReadProjection trick keeps EF namespaces out of Domain.
Read split: AppDbContext → ReadDbContextAdapter (AsNoTracking guarantee, Phase 2)
Every domain entity inherits AuditableEntity → CreatedBy · CreatedAt · ModifiedBy · ModifiedAt · RowVersion
SaveChanges interceptor stamps Created*/Modified* from ICurrentUser; recursive masking applied for PII (memory: phase-2)
"PlatformDb" → cross-cutting platform tables (decision D4: separate database)
User aggregate built end-to-end as canonical template (memory: project_phase_dbfirst_done).
In-process dispatch · Serilog · OpenTelemetry · App Insights
Each handler has a CancellationToken with a per-handler timeout · failures emit a metric and a structured log entry; the parent transaction is NOT rolled back (Phase 2 decision).
Serilog → structured JSON · enriched with CorrelationId, UserId, TenantId, ActivityId
OpenTelemetry → traces (HttpClient, EFCore, AspNetCore) + metrics (request duration, EF cmd time)
App Insights → frontend (web-vitals removed in Phase 3 polish — backend traces only)
• PII never logged — encryption at rest + recursive AuditMasker (Phase 2)
• Secrets sanitised by secretlint pre-commit · AWS EXAMPLE allowlist (memory)
Pre-merge gates: RestrictedAcrToBackchannel · PostLogoutRedirect validators · CorsSettingsValidator (Phase 2)
main.ts → providers → SignalStore → routes · standalone · OnPush · viewEncapsulation:'None'
HTTP interceptor chain (registration order — also execution order)
share()+finalize() ordering matters in the loading counter — wrong order leaks the in-flight count (memory: feedback_ui_phase6_gotchas).
Server state → @ngrx/signals SignalStore + HttpClient · cache via interceptor
DI cycle prevention → SyncCoordinator breaks Auth↔Telemetry circular dep (Phase 3)
Critical Angular gotchas captured in memory
• input.required read inside constructor → NG0950; use afterNextRender(), not queueMicrotask() (panel.component fix 2026-04-29)
• effect() that reads + writes the same signal loops forever — wrap reads or writes in untracked()
• [src] on iframe is a resource-URL context → bypass via DomSanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl with scheme allow-list
• @if-mounted drawer focus management uses queueMicrotask (defers DOM read until after the @if branch mounts)
• Backticks inside HTML comments inside template:`...` close the JS template literal mid-string
C.2 · UI CHROME (Phase E + F + F.7)
Each domain has a factory that returns NavbarConfig, FooterConfig, and theme tokens.
• DomainStore signal change → re-fetch nav config for the active domain
• AuthStore.user signal change → re-fetch (RBAC rules differ per user)
Server primary → BFF endpoint /api/navbar-config returns the menu the user IS allowed to see
Client filter → NavMenuComponent re-filters every render against the AuthStore.permissions signal
Hide, never disable — disallowed items don't render at all (no enumeration of hidden features)
[data-tone] attribute on the host (light / dark / accent) gates Tailwind v4 JIT-safe selectors
Single (navAction) dispatcher pattern — all menu interactions emit a discriminated NavActionEvent
Sass partials + sibling tailwind.css + cssLayer ordering
Tailwind directive lives in a sibling .css file because Sass 1.99 deprecates bare @import (Sass 3.0 hard error). Both files share the global :root token namespace.
_reset.scss · html/body, :focus-visible ring, @media print, chrome interaction guards
Real Tailwind v4 layer names — NOT the example tailwind-base/utilities (memory: feedback_primeng_csslayer_tailwind_v4).
_primeng-overrides.scss is layer-LESS so it beats PrimeNG defaults without !important.
Component styleUrl + viewEncapsulation:'None' → ::ng-deep is inert; never write it (use _primeng-overrides.scss for body-rendered widgets).
--ep-* on :root → @theme inline → Tailwind utilities + PrimeNG preset
Brand palette · 4 colors · 11-step scales · semantic aliases
Components reference aliases (class="bg-success") not scale indices — single edit re-skins every signal.
--ep-gradient-{primary, palmetto, jessamine, sunset, depth} · used for hero / marketing surfaces
• Generic by default — every component takes a [config] input + emits typed events; no business knowledge
• Token references via var(--ep-*) only — never raw hex in templates
